- Backlog Item ID: BI-260011
- Title: Formalize UX interaction and demo entry model
- Persona/Actor: Data manager and domain operator
- Value statement: For primary workspace users, establish a predictable interaction and entry model so the graph workspace is immediately usable and navigable in MVP.
- Context: User-experience decision phase for C1-C7.
- Problem statement: Without explicit UX-model decisions, graph-first workflows can start empty, navigation can fragment, and entry behavior can drift across environments.
- Expected outcome: Team can implement and validate a consistent graph-first UX with bootstrap search, workspace-centered navigation, side-panel editing, desktop focus, and minimal placeholders.
- Success metric(s):
  - C1-C7 decisions are accepted and encoded in formal artifacts.
  - UX and entry-flow requirements are testable and linked.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - ADR-260014 is created and accepted.
  - REQ-FR-260007, REQ-FR-260008, and REQ-CR-260003 are created and linked.
  - Existing REQ-OR-260001 is aligned to demo sign-in and linked.
  - Product backlog and backlog status include BI-260011 with traceability.
- Non-goals: Mobile-first UX, production multi-user authentication, and fully implemented non-core pages.
- Constraints: Preserve graph-first value, stack-neutral wording, and MVP-focused scope.
- Dependencies: Backend user guide and GraphQL contract.
- Risks/assumptions:
  - Risk: default bootstrap search may show unhelpful context if seed query is poorly chosen.
  - Risk: demo credentials could be misconfigured across environments.
  - Assumption: production auth will replace demo credentials without changing workspace UX primitives.
- Validation approach: End-to-end interaction checks plus environment/entry-flow verification.
- Priority score: 94
- Target window: MVP foundation
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260007.md, REQ-FR-260008.md, REQ-CR-260003.md, REQ-OR-260001.md
  - ADR: ADR-260014.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - Changeset: n/a
